channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: New Podcast Examines Inter-Agency Rivalry After Major Cocaine Interception in Operation Beech
item.description: A new podcast by The West Australian, titled Behind The White Line and hosted by investigative journalist Richard Baker, explores the dramatic events and consequences of Operation Beech. This sophisti...

item.title: Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda: Over 500 Suspected Cases and More Than 130 Deaths Reported
item.description: An Ebola virus outbreak involving the rare Bundibugyo strain has been reported in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, with the World Health Organization confirming more than 500 suspected cas...

item.title: Political and Social Instability in Britain Amid Cultural and Economic Challenges
item.description: The United Kingdom is currently facing a deep political and social crisis, with recent local elections highlighting challenges to both the Prime Minister and the entrenched ruling elite. British affai...

item.title: Maintenance works begin at Malankara dam, shutters opened to lower water level
item.description: The shutters of the Malankara dam, which is part of the Muvattupuzha Valley Irrigation Project (MVIP) in Idukki, Kerala, have been opened to initiate major repair and maintenance works. According to o...

item.title: Chimoney, Nigerian Fintech, Closes Operations Due to Insufficient Funding
item.description: Chimoney, one fintech wey dey Nigeria but also dey operate from Canada, don close shop after e no fit raise enough money to continue. The company, wey Uchi Uchibeke start for 2022, dey provide cross-b...

item.title: Prime Minister Mark Carney promotes nation-building projects and positive agenda during Vancouver speech amid pipeline discussions
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney addressed a business audience at the Greater Vancouver Board of Trade, emphasising a forward-looking nation-building agenda focused on what Canadians support rather than opp...

item.title: Government seeks Sh19.3 billion to tackle worsening drought and food insecurity
item.description: Kenya is facing a worsening drought situation that threatens millions of vulnerable citizens with hunger and humanitarian distress. The State Department for Special Programmes has appealed to Parliame...

item.title: CSA clarifies ticket allocations for Newlands New Year's Test amid fan frustration
item.description: Cricket South Africa (CSA) has come under criticism after tickets for the highly anticipated New Year’s Test between the Proteas and England at Newlands sold out within minutes. Only a small number of...

item.title: Family Calls for Reopened Investigation After Winnipeg Plasma Donor Dies of Heart Condition
item.description: Rodiyat Alabede, a 22-year-old international student, died after donating plasma at a for-profit Grifols centre in Winnipeg. Her family’s representative, Katherine Lanteigne, stated that Alabede had a...

item.title: Windsor man sentenced to nine months in jail for attempting to join neo-Nazi terrorist group Atomwaffen Division
item.description: A 23-year-old man from Windsor, Ontario, named Seth Bertrand has been sentenced to just under nine months in jail and three years of probation after being found guilty of participating in the activity...

item.title: House Democrats Move to Subpoena Trump Administration Officials Over $1.776 Billion Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: House Democrats, led by Rep. Jamie Raskin of Maryland, have moved quickly to increase oversight and pressure on the Trump administration regarding the newly established $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponizat...

item.title: Tanzania Cancels 40 Inactive Mineral Licenses and Reclaims 188,163 Acres for Development
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has taken strong steps in regulating its mining sector by cancelling 40 mineral exploration licenses covering a total of 188,163 acres. The decision came after repeated non-...

item.title: Sierra Leone accepts West African migrants deported from the US
item.description: On Wednesday, Sierra Leone received its first group of migrants deported from the United States under the former US administration’s immigration policies. A plane carrying nine individuals from Nigeri...

item.title: Troost-Ekong Says Winning Away Matches Crucial For AFCON 2027 Qualification
item.description: Former Super Eagles captain William Troost-Ekong don yan say the way teams take perform for away matches go fit really affect how dem go qualify for Africa Cup of Nations 2027. Troost-Ekong talk this ...

item.title: Ongoing Tourist Damage to Europe's Historic Landmarks Raises Local Concerns
item.description: Across Europe, a growing number of incidents involving tourists damaging historic landmarks is alarming both residents and heritage experts. Visitors have been observed carving initials into iconic st...

item.title: Mulamwah Misses Call from Daughter Keilah While Travelling to China, Shows Family Peace
item.description: Kenyan comedian Mulamwah recently shared a screenshot of a missed call from his daughter Keilah Oyando while preparing for a two-week seminar in China. Mulamwah, who is a father of three, had experien...

item.title: Zanzibar Lawmakers Criticise Outsourced Hospitals Over Poor Service and Staff Treatment
item.description: Zanzibar lawmakers have raised serious concerns about the performance of government hospitals that are run under private management contracts. Despite the government's investment in modern hospital in...

item.title: Annie Kilner hosts baby shower as she prepares to welcome fifth child, a daughter, with Kyle Walker
item.description: Annie Kilner, wife of footballer Kyle Walker, has shared images from her baby shower on Instagram as the couple await the arrival of their fifth child together, their first daughter. The 33-year-old p...

item.title: City of Cape Town plans affordable housing and mixed-use development on Civic Centre parking site
item.description: The City of Cape Town has approved preliminary plans to transform a municipal parking lot in the CBD's Foreshore precinct into an affordable housing and mixed-use development. Located next to the Civi...

item.title: Court dismisses union cases against St Mary’s Mumias Mission Hospital over staff disputes
item.description: The Employment and Labour Relations Court has dismissed two cases brought by the Kenya Union of Domestic, Hotels, Educational Institutions and Hospital Workers (Kudheiha) against St Mary’s Mumias Miss...
